meta.description: Assess calibration of diagnostic or prognostic prediction models by plotting predicted versus observed event probabilities. Inputs: predicted probabilities, binary outcomes. Outputs: calibration curve plot, Hosmer-Lemeshow test statistics, Brier score.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims, invented numeric outcomes, or unsupported conclusions were observed across the audit inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed inside research-analysis scope and did not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow uses an appropriate Cox-model plus bootstrap calibration design for time-to-event survival data.

code_usability.detail: Valid runs were executable and invalid inputs failed cleanly with actionable SKILL_* errors.

functional_suitability.note: The skill fully covers the promised Cox-model calibration workflow with aligned CLI options, outputs, references, and bundled test assets.

reliability.note: Validation, deterministic seeding, overwrite protection, and reproducibility logging are strong, though valid runs on the bundled cohort emit many repeated calibration warnings.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is clean and the documentation stays focused on the execution contract rather than implementation noise.

agent_usability.note: Trigger boundaries, examples, argument tables, testing commands, and troubleshooting references make the skill easy to invoke correctly.

human_usability.note: The workflow is easy to follow, but the repeated runtime warnings on successful small-cohort runs add some operator friction.

security.note: Inputs are validated, no unsafe execution primitives are present, and all file handling stays local and explicit.

maintainability.note: Responsibilities are cleanly separated across CLI, utilities, orchestration, and analysis helpers, with concise scripts and test helpers.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, clear boundaries, and idempotent reruns are all strong; the skill could still expose expected benign warning behavior more explicitly.

inputs.label: Default bundled calibration run
inputs.note: Completed successfully and produced the documented data, table, plot, and metadata files despite repeated benign calibration warnings on the tiny bundled cohort.

assertions.text: The documented main CLI path is runnable as described.
assertions.note: Rscript scripts/main.R completed successfully with the bundled test cohort.

assertions.text: The run emits the documented data, table, plot, and metadata artifacts.
assertions.note: calibration_data.qs, calibration_statistics.xlsx, calibration_curve.pdf, and session_info.txt were present.

assertions.text: The workflow records reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: session_info.txt captured parameters, the fixed seed, and package/session details.

assertions.text: The output stays within the stated calibration-analysis scope.
assertions.note: The run produced calibration artifacts only, without unrelated nomogram or ROC outputs.

inputs.label: Custom horizons + styled PDF
inputs.note: Accepted valid horizon and plotting overrides, preserved the standard output bundle, and recorded the custom values in session_info.txt.

assertions.text: The skill accepts valid horizon overrides and styling arguments.
assertions.note: The custom years, bootstrap count, and plot options all parsed successfully.

assertions.text: The output bundle remains stable under supported customization.
assertions.note: The same standard data, table, plot, and metadata outputs were still created.

assertions.text: Runtime metadata preserves the chosen overrides.
assertions.note: session_info.txt recorded the custom years, bootstrap count, geometry, colors, and title.

assertions.text: The customized run remains inside the skill's stated purpose.
assertions.note: The workflow still performed survival-model calibration only.

inputs.label: Missing-column rejection
inputs.note: Rejected the missing-feature request with a clear SKILL_MISSING_COLUMNS error; only empty output subdirectories were left behind.

assertions.text: The skill rejects a missing requested feature before model fitting.
assertions.note: Execution stopped during model-data preparation.

assertions.text: The failure message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error names the missing column directly.

assertions.text: The failure does not emit misleading analytical artifacts.
assertions.note: No .qs, .xlsx, .pdf, or session_info.txt files were produced for the invalid run.

assertions.text: The boundary behavior matches the documented contract.
assertions.note: The rejection is consistent with the error table and troubleshooting guide.

inputs.label: Documented smoke-test helper
inputs.note: The documented smoke-test helper executed successfully and rebuilt the bundled output directory with the expected artifact set.

assertions.text: The R smoke-test helper is runnable as documented.
assertions.note: Rscript tests/run_smoke_test.R executed successfully.

assertions.text: The helper rebuilds the documented output bundle.
assertions.note: tests/output contained the expected data, plot, table, and session files after execution.

assertions.text: The helper preserves reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: tests/output/session_info.txt was regenerated and remained non-empty.

assertions.text: The helper stays inside the skill's normal operational scope.
assertions.note: It simply wraps the main calibration workflow with bundled data.

inputs.label: Overwrite guard
inputs.note: The first seeded run succeeded and the second rerun was blocked cleanly by overwrite protection with an actionable SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER message.

assertions.text: The skill prevents silent replacement of an existing result bundle.
assertions.note: The second invocation was blocked before any files were overwritten.

assertions.text: The overwrite failure is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error message explicitly instructs the operator to use --overwrite.

assertions.text: The initial successful run still produced a complete audit trail.
assertions.note: The first invocation created the expected artifact bundle and session_info.txt.

assertions.text: The protection mechanism stays inside the skill's documented operating model.
assertions.note: It adds safety without changing the analysis workflow itself.

key_strengths: The skill now matches the target data-analysis template closely, with aligned CLI, references, test data, and reproducibility logging.
The execution contract is strong: valid runs complete, boundary failures are controlled, and the main workflow plus smoke-test helper are both runnable.
The skill maintains clear scope boundaries around survival-model calibration and avoids leaking into adjacent tasks like ROC analysis or nomogram construction.
The modular R implementation is maintainable and auditable, with concise scripts and explicit parameter capture in session_info.txt.

recommendations.title: Reduce repeated calibration warnings
recommendations.problem: Successful runs on the bundled small cohort emit many repeated 'one interval had < 2 observations' warnings, which can make healthy executions look unstable.
recommendations.root_cause: The current grouping strategy passed to rms::calibrate() is aggressive for a small bundled dataset and all warnings are surfaced verbatim.
recommendations.fix: Either adapt the group size for small-sample runs or document/filter repeated benign warnings so users can distinguish expected bootstrap noise from true failures.

recommendations.title: Avoid empty output scaffolds on early failure
recommendations.problem: The missing-column boundary case leaves empty data, table, and plot directories even though no analytical output is produced.
recommendations.root_cause: Output directories are created before required-column validation finishes.
recommendations.fix: Create the output scaffold after core input validation succeeds, or clean it up automatically when a fatal validation error occurs before analysis starts.
